claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 16 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 16 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 16 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm asking about my son Alex - he's 16 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Since Alex is 16 years old, I need to ask some additional questions to determine eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he attending school, college, or an approved training program?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he's still in education. He's doing his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you! 

**Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester - and Alex lives here at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

**Are you responsible for Alex's care?** (For example, do you provide for his food, clothes, and general living expenses?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm responsible for all of that - his food, clothes, everything he needs day to day.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information.

One more important question: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, Alex's other parent or another person?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✓ Alex is 16 years old and in approved education (doing A-levels at sixth form college)
✓ You live with him in the UK (Manchester)
✓ You're responsible for his care and provide for all his needs
✓ Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

Under UK rules, you normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Since Alex meets all these criteria, you are eligible to claim or continue receiving Child Benefit for him.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You should also inform the Child Benefit Office if Alex's circumstances change (for example, if he leaves education or starts working 24 hours or more per week).

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 16 years old and is in approved education (doing A-levels at sixth form college). Under the rules, you normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester), is responsible for his care including food, clothes and day-to-day expenses, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All the eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (16 years old), who lives with them in the UK. Alex is in approved education (A-levels at sixth form), the claimant is responsible for his care and provides for all his needs, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.

The claimant meets all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.
